task_completion.description: Navigate to ADAudit Plus reports and examine Failed Logon Activity to identify the brute-force target account.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing the ADAudit Plus web interface displaying Failed Logon reports or Local Logon Failure dashboards. The account 'svc_backup' should be visible with a disproportionately high count compared to other accounts.

task_completion.description: Navigate to Successful Logon or Recent Logon Activity reports to check if the targeted account was compromised.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing the ADAudit Plus web interface displaying Recent Logon Activity or Successful Logon reports. Evidence that the agent searched for or identified 'svc_backup' in successful logon events.

task_completion.description: Navigate to Group Membership Changes reports to check for privilege escalation of the compromised account.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing the ADAudit Plus web interface displaying Group Management or Membership Changes reports. Evidence that the agent found 'svc_backup' was added to the 'Administrators' group.

task_completion.description: Configure an alert profile named 'APT Campaign Detection' with Critical severity and email notification to soc-lead@corp.local.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots of the ADAudit Plus Alert Profiles configuration showing a new alert named 'APT Campaign Detection' with Critical severity, configured for failed logon threshold (5 in 10 minutes), with notification email 'soc-lead@corp.local'.

task_completion.description: Create a technician account 'forensic_lead' with the 'Auditor' role.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots of the ADAudit Plus Admin/Delegation or Technicians page showing a newly created account with username 'forensic_lead', full name 'Lead Forensic Analyst', and role 'Auditor'.

Screenshots of a text editor (e.g., Notepad) open with 'attack_campaign_report.txt' containing: the targeted username 'svc_backup', approximate number of failed attempts (~50), evidence of successful compromise, privilege escalation to 'Administrators', and remediation recommendations.

integrity.description: Agent used the ADAudit Plus web GUI to investigate reports rather than using CLI or API shortcuts.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent navigating the ADAudit Plus web interface in a browser to view failed logon reports, successful logon reports, and group change reports.

integrity.description: Agent actually viewed report data before writing findings — did not hallucinate the target account.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent viewing actual audit event data in the ADAudit Plus UI before opening a text editor to write the forensic report. The target username in the report matches what was visible in the ADAudit Plus reports.

integrity.description: Agent used a GUI text editor to write the forensic report.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent typing the report in a standard text editor like Notepad, rather than generating the file purely via command-line echo or redirection.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The primary attack target is 'svc_backup' with exactly 50 failed logon attempts. The attack chain is: brute-force (50 failed logons) -> successful compromise (1 successful logon) -> privilege escalation (added to Administrators and Security_Team groups). Noise accounts have much lower failed logon counts: rwilliams(8), dlee(6), jsmith(5), abrown(4), mjohnson(3). Benign group changes: mjohnson->IT_Support, rwilliams->Server_Admins. The technician must be 'forensic_lead' with role 'Auditor'. The alert must be named 'APT Campaign Detection' with Critical severity, threshold 5 failures in 10 minutes, email to soc-lead@corp.local. The forensic report must be saved as C:\Users\Docker\Desktop\attack_campaign_report.txt and should include the discovered username, failed attempt count, compromise evidence, privilege escalation details, and remediation recommendations.
